description: Enhance ContextAssembler to include tasks, progress, recent decisions, workspace file list, and plan.md content. Add token budgeting to truncate least-important sections when total exceeds budget.
steps: Modify kernel/context_assembler.py: Add _load_progress() method that reads memory/progress.yaml and formats iteration/tasks_done/tasks_total
Add _load_recent_decisions(count=5) method that reads last N lines from memory/decisions.jsonl
Add _load_workspace_manifest(max_entries=100) method that lists files in the workspace directory from state['workspace_path']
Add _load_plan() method that reads memory/plan.md content

Add these sections to assemble() in priority order: tasks (already exists as section 4) > progress > plan > workspace > decisions. Insert them between the current task section and the node prompt section

Add a token_budget parameter to assemble() (default 32000). Estimate tokens as len(text)//4. If total exceeds budget, truncate from least important (decisions first, then workspace, then plan, then progress) until within budget
Create tests/test_context_assembler_enhanced.py with tests for each new section, token budgeting truncation, missing files graceful handling
Run tests to confirm everything passes
acceptance_criteria: assemble() output includes progress section when progress.yaml exists
assemble() output includes recent decisions when decisions.jsonl has entries
assemble() output includes workspace manifest when workspace_path is set and directory exists
assemble() output includes plan.md content when file exists
Token budgeting truncates least important sections first
All new tests pass with coverage >= 90%
findings: All 23 new tests pass. Full suite 713 tests pass with 98.92% coverage. Added _load_progress, _load_recent_decisions, _load_workspace_manifest, _load_plan methods. Token budgeting removes sections in order: decisions, workspace, plan, progress. New sections inserted between current task and node prompt via _build_text helper.
